name: Qiongqi
description: Qiongqi, also known as the Hundun, is a malevolent creature in Chinese mythology, often described as one of the Four Perils (四凶). Its origins are somewhat ambiguous, with some accounts claiming it was banished to the mortal realm by the Yellow Emperor for its wickedness. Qiongqi is known for its insatiable appetite and destructive nature. It is said to devour humans, particularly those who are virtuous and righteous. The creature is also associated with chaos, deceit, and treachery. Legends depict Qiongqi as a symbol of moral corruption and a warning against succumbing to evil impulses. Its existence serves as a reminder of the constant struggle between good and evil and the importance of upholding moral principles. Qiongqi is a fearsome beast, embodying the worst aspects of human nature and serving as a cautionary tale in Chinese folklore. It is a powerful symbol of negativity and the destructive consequences of unchecked ambition and wickedness. The creature is a persistent threat, always lurking and waiting for an opportunity to sow discord and chaos. Some interpretations suggest that Qiongqi represents the darker aspects of the human psyche, the inner demons that must be constantly suppressed and overcome.
subtitle: Devourer of virtue and chaos.
appearance: Qiongqi's appearance varies across different accounts, but it is generally depicted as a large, monstrous beast. It often resembles a winged tiger or a hedgehog with wings. Some descriptions portray it as having the body of an ox, the quills of a hedgehog, and wings. It is usually described as having sharp claws, powerful jaws, and a terrifying roar. Its eyes are said to glow with a malevolent light.
habitat: Qiongqi is said to inhabit desolate and remote areas, such as mountains, forests, and wastelands. It prefers places where it can lurk in the shadows and prey on unsuspecting victims. Some legends place it in the Kunlun Mountains, a mythical mountain range in Chinese mythology.
behavior: Qiongqi is inherently evil and malicious. It actively seeks out opportunities to cause harm and destruction. It despises virtue and righteousness, and derives pleasure from tormenting and devouring those who are good. Qiongqi is cunning and deceptive, often using trickery and manipulation to achieve its goals. It is also incredibly greedy and selfish, always seeking to satisfy its insatiable appetite. The creature is prone to fits of rage and is easily provoked.

keyMyths.title: The Banished One
keyMyths.description: Qiongqi was banished from the heavens by the Yellow Emperor for its wickedness. This banishment marked the beginning of its reign of terror in the mortal realm, where it continues to spread chaos and destruction. It serves as a warning against moral decay.

sources.author: 袁珂 (Yuan Ke)
sources.work: 山海经校注 (Annotations to the Classic of Mountains and Seas)
sources.description: A modern annotation of the Classic of Mountains and Seas, providing interpretations of various mythical creatures.
sources.text: A modern annotation of the Classic of Mountains and Seas, providing interpretations of various mythical creatures.
